name: Texell
description_short: Texell Credit Union is a Texas-based credit union offering auto loans, mortgages, credit cards, checking/savings accounts, and insurance products with competitive rates and 5-star financial stability ratings.
description_long:
Texell Credit Union was founded in 1948 and has grown into a major financial institution serving Texas members. The credit union consistently achieves five-star ratings from Bauer Financial, demonstrating strong financial stability and operational soundness. With over 92,000 free ATMs worldwide and deposit insurance up to $500,000, Texell positions itself as a member-focused alternative to traditional banks.

Texell offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including auto loans (as low as 3.99% APR), fixed mortgages (as low as 5.49% APR), home equity lines (as low as 5.24% APR), personal loans (as low as 9.49% APR), certificates of deposit (up to 3.60% APY), credit cards with reward points, checking and savings accounts, and commercial/SBA loans. The credit union also provides insurance solutions through Texell Insurance and offers a car buying service powered by TrueCar. Digital banking is available across mobile, tablet, and desktop platforms with instant-approval online loan applications.

Texell distinguishes itself through several member benefits: membership is open to all Texans (not limited by employer or location), competitive APR/APY rates, a debit rewards program described as "one of the only debit rewards programs around," and personalized service emphasizing member status rather than customer relationships. The institution offers youth accounts for financial education and hosts community events like Shred Day for identity protection awareness. They also provide financial literacy content through their blog covering money management, home ownership, fraud prevention, and insurance topics.

As a credit union, Texell serves as a legitimate financial institution offering standard banking and lending products. Prospective members should note that while the website emphasizes competitive rates and five-star ratings, actual approval depends on creditworthiness, and some featured rates may require excellent credit. The institution is regulated as a credit union and maintains NCUA insurance for member deposits. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Home equity lines of credit (as low as 5.24% APR)
Credit cards with reward points on credit and debit transactions
Checking and savings accounts with online account opening
Mortgage services through Texell Home Loans
Digital banking with mobile, tablet, and desktop access
Car buying service powered by TrueCar
pros: Consistently earns five-star ratings from Bauer Financial, indicating strong financial stability
Membership open to all Texans with no employer/location restrictions
Access to 92,000+ free ATMs worldwide plus debit rewards program
Competitive rates across multiple products: auto loans from 3.99% APR, mortgages from 5.49% APR, CDs up to 3.60% APY
Deposit insurance up to $500,000 on all deposit accounts (exceeds standard NCUA coverage for members with multiple account types)
Instant-approval online loan application process for auto and personal loans
Comprehensive member benefits including youth accounts, financial literacy blog, and community fraud prevention events
cons: Featured 'as low as' rates require qualification and likely require excellent credit; actual APRs may be significantly higher
Geographic limitation: membership restricted to Texas residents only
Limited transparency on website regarding eligibility requirements, application timeline, or typical approval rates
Credit union membership and approval depend on creditworthiness; no mention of options for credit-challenged applicants
Website does not clearly disclose late fees, minimum balance requirements, or other account restrictions
best_for: Texas residents seeking competitive rates on auto loans and mortgages from a financially stable institution
Borrowers with good-to-excellent credit looking for instant-approval online lending with low APRs
Members interested in debit card rewards and access to a large ATM network combined with traditional banking services
diagnosis: Texell Credit Union is best for Texas residents with good-to-excellent credit seeking competitive rates and personalized service from a financially stable credit union. The main caveat is that 'as low as' rates require strong creditworthiness, and prospective members should verify they meet eligibility requirements before applying; the institution is not designed for credit repair or credit-building services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
